title: Conditional Formatting on Forms
excerpt: Workforms  offers conditional formatting (ie. if a certain answer is chosen, show/hide related questions), but is missing other key features I need. I’ve been using Superforms which does not have conditional formatting b…

posts.author: JacksonBarry (Jackson Barry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Workforms offers conditional formatting (ie. if a certain answer is chosen, show/hide related questions), but is missing other key features I need. I’ve been using Superforms which does not have conditional formatting but does support connected and mirror columns and updating of existing items.

Does anyone know of an option that can offer all of this features? #jotform?

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes — you can achieve all of that with The PDF Maker inside monday.com.

It combines everything you’re looking for:

  • Conditional Logic — Show or hide questions based on previous answers so your forms adapt dynamically.

  • Connected & Mirror Columns — Map form responses directly to connected boards or mirrored data, keeping everything synced.

  • Update Existing Items — Instead of always creating new ones, you can update existing monday items based on form responses.

  • Plus More — Collect eSignatures, attach uploaded files, and automatically generate PDFs after submission — all within monday.com.

So instead of switching between tools like WorkForms or Superforms, you can handle conditional forms, updates, and document generation in one place with The PDF Maker.

Learn more: How to use the forms feature in monday.com
